commit.message:
feat: intelligent reminder lead time based on task type

- Meeting/appointment: remind 30 min before (not at start time)
- Payment/bills: remind 3 days before
- Deadline/submission: remind 1 day before
- Travel/flight: remind 2 hours before
- Medical appointment: remind 1 hour before
- Other timed tasks: remind 15 min before
- No time specified: remind at 9am on due date

Also: reminder confirmation always shows actual remind time vs due time

commit.message:
refactor: code review — security, performance, and organisation fixes

Security: validate interruptionPhrase against known safe values in
parseResult to prevent prompt-injection into spoken output; add MODEL
constant to pedantEngine; selective Zustand selectors in App to reduce
render surface area.

Performance: memoize FlatList renderItem in HistoryCards with
useCallback so React.memo on HistoryCard is not defeated by new
function references.

Organisation: move SafeAreaProvider into App.tsx; simplify index.ts to
minimal registerRootComponent entry point.

Clean code: remove unused useEffect import from useListeningPipeline;
extract MODEL constant in pedantEngine.

Docs: update README — correct SDK version (54), update stack table
(expo-audio replaces expo-speech-recognition), add test input usage
instructions, clarify speech recognition constraint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Integrate recompiler into block processing with backend switching

Add PvmInstance abstraction (GREY_PVM env var) supporting three modes:
interpreter (default), recompiler, and compare (per-instruction lockstep
verification). Fix ~15 classes of recompiler codegen bugs found via
compare mode: three-register aliasing, flags clobber from xor-zeroing,
x86-64 immediate sign extension, sbrk semantics, signed load extension,
mul_upper stack offsets, and more. All 311+ tests and 101/101 conformance
blocks now pass with both interpreter and recompiler backends.

commit.message:
docs: execute_sql SQLバリデーションをブラックリスト方式に変更

F7.1/F9.1の要件をホワイトリスト方式からブラックリスト方式に変更。
DELETE に加え ALTER, GRANT, REVOKE, VACUUM, ANALYZE, CREATE TABLE（非TEMP）,
CREATE/DROP INDEX 等を拒否対象に追加。CREATE/DROP は後続トークンで安全性を判定。

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename project from Pearl to Conch

- Binary: pl → conch
- Data directory: .pearl/ → .conch/
- Issue ID prefix: pl- → conch-
- PyPI package: pearl-cli → conch-cli
- Source files: pearl.{hpp,cpp} → conch.{hpp,cpp}
- Classes: Pearl/PearlError/PearlConfig → Conch/ConchError/ConchConfig
- SQL macros: pl_* → conch_*
- Test script: test_pl.sh → test_conch.sh
- All docs, workflows, and build system updated

176 tests pass.

Fix 4 bugs: special token crash, SFT padding, FSDP no_sync, rotary device

- Fix tiktoken ValueError on <|endoftext|> in web text: use disallowed_special=()
- Fix SFT padding: pad inputs with 0 (valid token) not -100 (embedding crash)
- Fix FSDP gradient accumulation: add no_sync() for micro-steps to avoid
  redundant all-reduce communication (7x speedup for grad_accum=8)
- Fix rotary cache rebuild: create tensors on correct device to avoid
  CPU/GPU mismatch when sequence length exceeds initial cache

commit.message:
fix: scope stock chart view to prediction cycle and enable MACD

Stock 15m/1h charts were showing the entire trading day from pre-market,
causing massive whitespace and squished prediction bands. Now scopes the
visible range to the current prediction cycle (Block 1 start to Block 2
end) with 30% candle lead-in context. Also bumps Yahoo Finance range
from 1d to 5d for 15m/1h intervals so MACD has enough candles (34+)
for its warm-up period.

Includes stocks integration: asset selector, market status hook, extended
hours filter, signal colors, dynamic block legend, and prediction card updates.

commit.message:
Fix print scaling: use margin:0 with grid padding for reliable page fit

Safari overrides @page margin:10mm with a larger value (~17mm) to
accommodate its own headers/footers, shrinking the content area to
~263mm — not enough for 4 rows at 69mm each.

Fix: set @page margin:0 (suppresses browser H&F, gives full 297mm),
and apply padding:10mm directly on .print-grid with box-sizing:border-box.
This gives consistent 10mm margins on every page (including page 2+)
and guarantees each grid fills exactly one A4 page.

Add CombinedScan single-pass PBF extraction, scan progress tracking, flatten AnalyzeRegion

- Add combined/ plugin architecture: 7 extractor plugins (amenities, population,
  roads, routes, parks, buildings, boundaries) run in one apply_file() pass
- Add ExtractCategoryResult event facet to parse per-category results from CombinedScan
- Flatten AnalyzeRegion workflow: 1 CombinedScan replaces 10 composed workflows (15+ scans)
- Add ScanProgressTracker: element-count-based progress at 1/8 file-size milestones
- Add progress tracking to all PBF-scanning handlers (type filter, osmose verifier,
  zoom graph, postgis importer, boundary extractor)
- Remove indent=2 from GeoJSON output for 10-30x faster finalize
- Remove redundant SimpleHandler classes from extractors replaced by CombinedScan

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove dynamic JSON-LD schema generation from blog posts

Remove automatic Article and BreadcrumbList schema generation that was happening
dynamically in the browser. These schemas were being injected via JavaScript
on every blog post load.

Changes:
- Removed Article schema generation (data-schema='dynamic-article')
- Removed BreadcrumbList schema generation (data-schema='dynamic-breadcrumb')
- Removed custom Sanity JSON-LD schema injection
- Kept cleanup functions (they will handle any remaining schemas gracefully)
- Preserved existing meta tag generation
- Preserved canonical URL and other SEO elements

Impact:
- Blog posts no longer inject schemas dynamically
- Schemas are not added via JavaScript (client-side)
- Google and other search engines won't see these dynamic schemas
- Cleaner HTML with no runtime schema injection
- Better performance (less DOM manipulation)

Note: This removes client-side schema generation. For SEO, schemas should be
generated server-side during build or pre-render, not injected via JavaScript.

commit.message:
Fix SSL warnings, add security headers, and improve caching

next.config.mjs — security headers on every route:
- Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS, 1yr, includeSubDomains, preload)
  forces browsers to always use HTTPS — eliminates SSL warning repeat
- X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff — prevents MIME sniffing attacks
- X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN — blocks clickjacking
- X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block
- Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
- Permissions-Policy: disables camera/mic/tracking APIs
- Cache-Control: immutable for /_next/static, 7d for images/fonts,
  no-store for /api routes

proxy.ts — unified request handling:
- HTTP → HTTPS redirect (301) in production
- www → non-www canonical redirect (301) — fixes split SSL origin warning
- Cache-Control: public s-maxage=60 stale-while-revalidate=300 for
  public pages (CDN edge caching)
- Cache-Control: no-store for /crm and /api (always fresh)

app/layout.tsx — removed global force-dynamic
- Was forcing every single page to SSR on every request, defeating all
  caching. CRM and API routes already set their own dynamic = force-dynamic.

commit.message:
ci: add GitHub Actions — Claude Code review, autofix, triage, audit

Ported from jadecli/jade-cofounder, adapted for this repo:
- ci.yml: build (tsc) + test (vitest) on push/PR to main
- staff-review.yml: claude-code-action PR review (SDK correctness, types, WebMCP, zod v4)
- ci-autofix.yml: auto-fix on CI failure for non-main branches
- issue-triage.yml: label + comment on new issues
- weekly-audit.yml: Monday 9AM UTC audit for stubs, SDK misuse, S-team gaps
- REVIEW.md: review guidelines for claude-code-action context

Requires: CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN secret set on the repo.

feat: add X and XX penalty stroke buttons to round club tracking

Adds two penalty buttons to the club grid in clubs tracking mode:
- X records a single penalty stroke (one 'X' entry in clubs_used)
- XX records two penalty strokes at once (two 'X' entries)

Uses 'X' rather than 'P' to avoid confusion with the Putter button,
and implies an erroneous situation consistent with golf penalty rules.

Domain tests document that penalty strokes are valid HoleResult entries.
Acceptance scenarios cover both X and XX button behaviour via Playwright.

commit.message:
fix: duel coin animation — CSS idle instead of WebGL, remove overflow clipping

Use lightweight CSS coin spin for idle duel phase instead of mounting
a Three.js Canvas per card. Three.js only mounts for merge/reveal phases.
Prevents GPU flooding when many duels are active (browser WebGL context
limit ~8-16). Remove overflow-hidden on duel card to stop border-radius
clipping the 3D coin ("sharp corners" bug).
